values.name: Googleyness
values.description: A combination of traits Google looks for: intellectual humility, comfort with ambiguity, bias for action, and collaborative nature.
behavioral_signals: Thrives in ambiguity — doesn't need everything spelled out
Intellectually humble — open to being wrong, eager to learn
Collaborative — defaults to working with others, shares credit
Fun to work with — positive energy without being fake
Does the right thing — ethical compass, user-first thinking
Pushes back thoughtfully — disagrees with data, not ego

values.name: General Cognitive Ability (GCA)
values.description: Ability to learn and solve novel problems, not just apply known patterns.
behavioral_signals: Asks insightful questions that reframe the problem
Adapts approach when initial plan doesn't work
Reasons from first principles, not just pattern matching

values.name: Leadership
values.description: Emergent leadership — stepping up when needed, not positional authority.
behavioral_signals: Leads when appropriate, follows when appropriate
Navigates ambiguity and brings clarity to teams
Takes ownership of hard problems others avoid

values.name: Role-Related Knowledge
values.description: Deep technical expertise relevant to the role.
behavioral_signals: Strong fundamentals, not just surface-level knowledge
Can go deep on relevant topics when probed
Applies knowledge practically, not just theoretically
